commit.message:
Fix pivoting-agent lint: put search_skills/list_skills on same line as "do not"

The lint test checks per-line for search_skills()/list_skills() references
and only exempts lines containing "do not" or "never call". The prohibition
was split across two lines, so the line with the function names didn't match.

commit.message:
Add pivot-aware scanning to network-recon, improve dashboard agent lifecycle

network-recon skill:
- Add "Pivot Mode" section with two-phase scanning approach
- Phase 1: fast host discovery on pivot host (ARP, ping sweep, DNS zone,
  single-port proxychains sweep)
- Phase 2: targeted nmap through proxychains on known-live hosts only
- Document nmap MCP server limitation (no proxychains support)
- Add static nmap alternative for speed-critical scenarios
- Add pivot-specific troubleshooting entries

Dashboard (tail-agent.py):
- Auto-discover new agents from tasks directory (no .dashboard file needed)
- Red *stopped* indicator in pane header for dead agents
- Detect agent completion via JSONL final message + 5s mtime debounce
- Detect killed agents (TaskStop) via 30s mtime staleness
- Keep stopped agents visible instead of auto-removing
- Space bar toggles agents in/out from browser overlay

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: preserve discovery scroll position on back navigation

Cache articles and scroll position in a module-level variable when
Discovery unmounts. On back navigation (POP), restore cached state
instead of re-fetching, then restore scroll position after render.
On forward navigation (PUSH, e.g. logo click), fetch fresh data.

Reverts new-tab approach in favor of same-tab with state caching.

commit.message:
Fix root cause: restorePlayingRow now CSS-only, remove debug logs

Root cause: restorePlayingRow() was called during genre switch re-renders
and recreated EQ overlays with _eqOverlayHTML, which could trigger
playback side-effects. Now it ONLY adds .playing and .eq-active CSS
classes to the track row. No _activateBar, no _eqOverlayHTML, no
iframe creation, no embed area manipulation.

Also removed all diagnostic console.logs from player.js and main.js.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

直し/学習のAPI 3回→2回に圧縮: diff分析と指示集更新を統合

profileContext未渡し時は従来の3回フローにフォールバック。

- analyzeFeedbackWithClaude: 第4引数profileContext追加
- updateAdvancedProfile: persona_definition_next直接適用の分岐追加
- getProfileAndPrompt: DB1回でプロンプト+コンテキスト一括取得

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix nodiscard warnings in sampling_impl.cu for HIP/CUDA API calls

Add (void) casts to all HIP/CUDA API calls (cudaEventRecord, cudaMemset,
cudaMemcpyAsync, cudaMalloc, cudaFree, etc.) that have hipError_t return
values marked with nodiscard attribute. This explicitly indicates we are
intentionally ignoring the return values, suppressing 46 compiler warnings
during HIP kernel compilation for gfx1102 (AMD Radeon PRO W7800).

Fixes: 46 warnings in sampling_impl.cu compilation
Target: gfx1102 (RDNA3)

task: update error-messages-unsanitized-markdown with research findings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(str): assign orders/sales to start date; prorate only flow metrics

Orders and sales are outcome events tied to a specific moment — they
must not be divided across days (round(1/7) = 0 silently drops orders).

New behaviour:
  - spend, impressions, clicks: prorated evenly across all days in range
  - orders, sales/revenue: full total on the START date, 0 on subsequent days

This preserves totals exactly without making false assumptions about
when conversions occurred. Verified against Excel: orders and sales now
match to the unit/cent across TBS, repro_books_uae, and arya_vastu_20.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(timing): fix BaZi dead code + test normalization + cap comments

Critical fixes from code review:
1. BaZi window was dead code — bazi_chart in DB omits current_pillar
   (changes every decade). Now calls compute_dayun_pillars() at match
   time to get live current_pillar so 大運配偶星旺衰 actually fires.
2. Normalization test used /120.0 but implementation uses /140.0.
   Fixed test to use /140.0 with a non-trivial BaZi input so the
   assertion is actually meaningful (non-zero score).

Important fixes:
3. Per-person window cap comments said "50" but code uses min(60).
   Updated test docstrings and assertions to 60.
4. Overlap bonus test docstring said ">= 20" but threshold is >= 25.
5. test_missing_current_pillar: assertion expected window_a==0 but
   流年天干 (annual stem) fires regardless of 大運 pillar. Corrected
   to assert no "大運" signals and window_a <= 10.
6. Move timing_engine + compute_dayun_pillars imports to module top

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Allow ODH dev flags to override MR upstream temp dev features (#6182)

* feat(model-registry): allow ODH dev feature flags to override upstream temp dev features

This adds support for ODH's dev feature flags modal to control the upstream
temp dev features (CatalogHuggingFaceApiKey, RegistryStorage). The flags now
appear in the "Dev Flags" tab and can be toggled alongside other dev flags.

Implementation uses a context/provider pattern that reads from ODH's session
storage, allowing the modal-set values to override the localStorage-based
defaults while preserving the console toggle functionality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Mike Turley <mike.turley@alum.cs.umass.edu>

* feat(model-registry): use human-readable display names for upstream dev flags

Update the dev flags to show "KF MR Upstream: Catalog HuggingFace API Key"
and "KF MR Upstream: Registry OCI Storage" in the ODH dev flags modal
instead of the raw technical key names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Mike Turley <mike.turley@alum.cs.umass.edu>

* fix(model-registry): read ODH dev flags from session storage, not localStorage

The useBrowserStorage hook signature is (key, defaultValue, jsonify, isSessionStorage).
We were only passing 3 params, so isSessionStorage defaulted to false (localStorage).
ODH stores dev flags in session storage, so we need to pass true as the 4th param.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Mike Turley <mike.turley@alum.cs.umass.edu>

* feat(model-registry): instant dev flag updates via custom event

ODH now dispatches 'odh-dev-flags-changed' custom event when dev flags change.
Model-registry listens for this event and immediately re-reads from sessionStorage,
eliminating the ~10 second delay that occurred because storage events don't fire
for same-tab sessionStorage changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Mike Turley <mike.turley@alum.cs.umass.edu>

* refactor(model-registry): rename feature flag types to use "Odh" and "Overrides" naming

Renamed to make it clear these are ODH-specific overrides:
- TempDevFeatureFlagsContext → OdhDevFeatureFlagOverridesContext
- TempDevFeatureFlagsOverrides → OdhDevFeatureFlagOverrides
- useTempDevFeatureFlagsOverrides → useOdhDevFeatureFlagOverrides
- TempDevFeatureFlagsProviderExtension → OdhDevFeatureFlagOverridesProviderExtension
- isTempDevFeatureFlagsProviderExtension → isOdhDevFeatureFlagOverridesProviderExtension
- model-registry.feature-flags/provider → model-registry.feature-flag-overrides/provider

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Mike Turley <mike.turley@alum.cs.umass.edu>

* refactor(model-registry): rename feature-flags.ts to feature-flag-overrides.ts

Consistent with the "overrides" naming convention used throughout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Mike Turley <mike.turley@alum.cs.umass.edu>

---------

Signed-off-by: Mike Turley <mike.turley@alum.cs.umass.edu>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Runner GPS: try all user farm IDs for orchard matching

User may have access to multiple farms. Previously only tried
farmIds[0], which could miss orchards on other farms. Now iterates
all farm IDs from runnerapp_farm_ids until a match is found.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: kill entire process tree on shutdown to prevent orphaned claude sessions

On Windows, child.kill() only terminates cmd.exe but leaves the piped
claude process running. Now uses taskkill /F /T /PID to kill the full
process tree. On Unix, uses process groups (detached + negative PID).

Also adds exit handlers for all shutdown scenarios: SIGINT, SIGTERM,
SIGHUP (terminal close), process.exit, uncaughtException, and
unhandledRejection — ensuring child processes are always cleaned up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(mobile-storefront): App Settings screen + restore account deletion (#532)

* Warden: replace select(*) with specific columns in invoice route

Replaces the generic `select('*')` query in `apps/web/src/app/api/orders/[id]/invoice/route.ts` with explicit column selection to prevent over-fetching and adhere to the project's strict data integrity guidelines.
- Uses `ORDER_COLUMNS` from `@/lib/order-queries` for the orders query.
- Explicitly lists columns for `order_items` and `order_tax_subtotals` queries.
- Safely types the potentially nested or array response from the `merchants` relation.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Vercel token passing in deploy workflow

Modifies `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` to pass the `VERCEL_TOKEN` secret to the Vercel CLI via the environment variable instead of using the `--token` flag. This fixes CI build errors where the token passed to the CLI was considered invalid.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: pass VERCEL_TOKEN via shell environment variables instead of direct interpolation

Direct interpolation of GitHub secrets into shell commands can cause errors or dropping characters if the token contains special characters. This commit maps the secret to an environment variable and uses `$VERCEL_TOKEN` within the command line flag `--token=$VERCEL_TOKEN` to pass it safely to `vercel`.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(mobile-storefront): address CodeRabbit review feedback

- Add error handling for push notification toggle
- Remove redundant nested <Text> wrappers in section titles
- Enable haptics on Android (expo-haptics supports both platforms)
- Use SPACING constants where applicable
- Add signOut failure resilience after account deletion
- Add mobile network error patterns (fetch failed, ECONNREFUSED, ENETUNREACH)
- Wrap test callbacks in act() for consistency
- Add afterEach mock cleanup in useColorScheme tests
- Use path alias in settings-store test
- Validate downloaded Chrome path in CI workflow
- Remove redundant colorScheme fallback in delete-account screen
- Add AsyncStorage mock to jest setup
- Remove duplicate deleteAccount declarations in auth-store

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address second round of CodeRabbit review feedback

- Remove redundant setState calls in useColorScheme tests (beforeEach handles it)
- Rename misleading test name in settings-store
- Add error check after puppeteer download, simplify find pattern in CI
- Add merchant null guard in invoice route

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mobile-storefront): third round CodeRabbit review fixes

- Simplify mock cleanup with jest.clearAllMocks() in delete-account tests
- Export SettingsState interface for consumer typing
- Adjust disabled delete button text color for better contrast
- Add test for undefined identities edge case in hasAppleProvider

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): restore jest.spyOn mocks with restoreAllMocks in beforeEach

jest.clearAllMocks() only clears call history but doesn't restore spied
functions. Adding restoreAllMocks() ensures Linking spies are properly
restored between tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): clean up invoice route — remove 'use server', simplify merchant cast, log tax errors

- Remove incorrect 'use server' directive (route handlers are not Server Actions)
- Simplify merchant extraction since merchants!inner guarantees a single object
- Log errors from order_tax_subtotals query instead of silently ignoring them

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): add runtime merchant validation and fail on tax subtotal errors

- Replace unsafe double type assertion with runtime shape validation
- Return 500 instead of proceeding with missing tax data for VAT-registered
  merchants, since approximate tax subtotals could produce non-compliant invoices

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): quote VERCEL_TOKEN in deploy workflow, sanitize merchant error log

- Double-quote $VERCEL_TOKEN refs to fix shellcheck SC2086 (actionlint failure)
- Log only structural info (type, isArray, isNull) instead of full merchant
  object to avoid leaking sensitive data in production logs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api,ci): validate merchant fields at runtime, standardize token syntax

- Add runtime checks for required merchant fields (id, user_id, business_name)
  before casting to MerchantData, protecting invoice generation from malformed data
- Standardize deploy.yml preview job to use shell "$VERCEL_TOKEN" syntax
  matching the production job

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): cast validated md variable instead of unchecked merchantData

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): validate orderId with Zod, scope query to user, normalize merchant join

- Validate route param as UUID with Zod before any DB access (400 on invalid)
- Add .eq('merchants.user_id', user.id) to push authorization to query level
- Normalize merchant join payload: accept array and extract [0] for resilience
- Keep post-fetch user_id check as defense-in-depth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add thresholdPercent for percentage-based diff tolerance

Adds a new `thresholdPercent` config option (default: 0.5%) alongside the
existing absolute pixel `threshold`. A test passes when either threshold is
satisfied, making it resilient to font antialiasing and subpixel rendering
differences across environments.

Also includes: TUI error/warning sublines, keyboard scrolling (j/k,
Ctrl+U/D, PageUp/PageDown), keyboard thread fix, summary bar shortcuts,
dimension mismatch handling (width warns, height silent), and directory
cleanup before test runs.

commit.message:
Convert all photo galleries to carousels, fix services process layout

- Replace stacked photo grid galleries with sliding carousels on all pages
- Add carousel JS with prev/next buttons, dots, auto-play, touch swipe
- Fix process-step CSS conflict (duplicate definition causing broken layout)
- Pages are now much shorter and cleaner

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: china-hilight-website
repository.description: China Hi-Light Events & Travel - Corporate Website

commit.message:
fix: cron_rebuild.sh universe IDX200 → IDX250

Was silently rebuilding with the smaller IDX200 universe every day at
16:30 WIB (after market close), overwriting manual IDX250 builds and
causing the screener to show ~197 stocks instead of ~224.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: bandarmology-screener
repository.description: Indonesian stock screener using bandarmology (institutional broker flow analysis) to identify smart money accumulation across IDX200 universe

commit.message:
feat(idea-machina): move Validointi from SOIHTU to VALO stage

Move the validation force module from SOIHTU's STRATEGIA box to a dedicated
VALIDOINTI CollapsibleSection in the VALO stage with yellow theme colors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: output directory picker for audio optimization

- audio.html: add output dir input field (visible after track load)
- audioHooks: pre-fill input with project dir, pass outputDir to optimizeAudio()
- audioEnhancement: loadAudioTracks() returns projectDir, optimizeAudio() accepts outputDir
- backendAPI: downloadOptimizedFile() writes to user-specified outputDir
- premiereProAPI: importOptimizedClips() accepts outputDir, passes to download

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: restore and update CSYT deploy bug report with root cause analysis
